title: Why resisting Trump has galvanized Black Democrats as the midterms approach
description: High primary turnout among Black voters in the South has given some in the party hope for upset wins in the region.

title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.

title: A bridge to Canada may be blocked by the Trump administration
description: The Gordie Howe bridge spans the most important border crossing between the U.S. and Canada. President Donald Trump has said he doesn't want it open yet.

title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
description: President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: Four Black women. Nine degrees. Not one steady paycheck.
description: The president promised to save “Black jobs,” but his policies have resulted in fresh pain for the Black middle class as the employment gap widens.

title: Why the nation’s founding document is marred by stains, a mysterious handprint
description: The physical journey of the Declaration of Independence tells its own story. Its imperfections and endurance reflect those of the America it helped create.

title: Politics chat: SCOTUS hands Trump a win, GOP diverges on housing bill
description: The contours of the midterms are coming into focus and there appears to be strategic daylight between the administration and Congressional Republicans on what to run on.

title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.

title: GOP candidate with same name as senator can stay on Alaska ballot, judge rules
description: Supporters of incumbent Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan say a challenger with the same name is not running in “good faith,” but a judge says that is not an issue.

title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to 1,430; U.S. to send additional aid
description: An expected nine-figure monetary aid package comes on top of $150 million that Washington already committed to disaster relief in Venezuela after two massive earthquakes.

title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma state trooper to head ICE
description: President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.

title: Trump reveals new images of passport with his portrait to mark America’s 250th
description: The State Department said the new document “boldly honors” the president and the nation’s founding. The rendering differed from previous images of the passport in several ways.

title: Examining Trump's interest in the SAVE America Act
description: President Trump has an interest in a piece of voting legislation, called the SAVE America Act, that is not shared by all of his Republican colleagues on Capitol Hill.

title: Week in Politics: Trump and bipartisan housing bill, John Bolton; Democratic socialists
description: President Trump canceled a signing for major housing legislation, former National Security Advisor John Bolton pled guilty to mishandling classified documents, and Democratic Socialists made gains.

title: What would George Washington say? It's a busy year for people who portray him
description: America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.

title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
description: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.
